# Remotion Timeline | Remotion | Make videos programmatically

[Skip to main content](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/timeline#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Copy page

On this page

![Remotion Timeline Banner](https://www.remotion.dev/img/remotion-timeline-banner.jpg)  
  

A paid customizable timeline component that integrates seamlessly with [Remotion Player](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/player)
.  
It's perfect for builders who need a layer-based editing interface for their video editing app.

You can buy the timeline component on [remotion.pro](https://www.remotion.pro/timeline)
.

[**Demo**\
\
Video walkthrough of the timeline component](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/timeline/demo)
[**Setup**\
\
Installation and configuration guide](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/timeline/setup)
[**Usage**\
\
Component integration and state management](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/timeline/usage)
[**FAQ**\
\
Frequently asked questions](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/timeline/faq)

Key Features[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/timeline#key-features "Direct link to Key Features")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Interface:

*   Multiple track support with drag-and-drop functionality (between and within tracks)
*   Timeline item selection and manipulation
*   Keyboard shortcuts for common actions
*   `CanvasComposition` for rendering the timeline state in the Remotion Player
*   Zoomable timeline and `<ZoomSlider>` component to control the zoom level

Customization:

*   Theming with variables (Tailwind CSS V4 and V3)
*   Extensible state management

License[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/timeline#license "Direct link to License")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The source code of the Remotion Timeline is governed by the [Remotion License](https://github.com/remotion-dev/remotion/blob/main/LICENSE.md)
 and [its own specific license](https://www.remotion.pro/timeline?tab=license)
.

*   Remotion, as an underlying technology of the Remotion Timeline, is free to use for individuals and small companies. If you fall into this category, you can buy the Remotion Timeline as a one-time purchase. If you are a larger company, you must subscribe to the [Company License](https://www.remotion.pro/license)
    . Read the [Terms & Conditions](https://www.remotion.pro/terms)
    .
    
*   The Remotion Timeline License [can be seen in the Remotion Store Page](https://www.remotion.pro/timeline)
     as well as accessible through the dedicated GitHub repository after purchase.
    

*   [Key Features](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/timeline#key-features)
    
*   [License](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/timeline#license)

---

# Support Policy | Remotion | Make videos programmatically

[Skip to main content](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/support#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Copy page

On this page

The Remotion team provides **no guarantee** for support, and you should not hold the expectation to receive any help.

However, we welcome you to ask questions and invest a lot of time into answering them.  
Follow the steps below if have a question that you would like to get answered.

How to ask for help[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/support#how-to-ask-for-help "Direct link to How to ask for help")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[See here](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/get-help)
 for channels where you can ask for help as well as for best practices for asking questions.

Prioritized support[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/support#prioritized-support "Direct link to Prioritized support")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Company License customers can get a ![](https://www.remotion.dev/img/blue-heart.png) Company License badge on Discord for their team members. The badge can be activated on [remotion.pro](https://www.remotion.pro/)
.

If posted in a public channel, the Remotion team will try to prioritize your question, albeit no guarantee of support is given.

We still expect company license subscribers to follow the best practices for asking questions outlined above.

Paid consulting[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/support#paid-consulting "Direct link to Paid consulting")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Our [Remotion Experts](https://www.remotion.dev/experts)
 are available for technical advice and guidance. With their extensive experience in creating Remotion projects, they will surely be of great help.

The only way to receive consulting support from the Remotion team is through the [Enterprise License](https://remotion.pro/license)
.

When we can't help[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/support#when-we-cant-help "Direct link to When we can't help")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

We don't provide support if:

*   the guidelines above were not followed
*   low effort was put into first solving the problem yourself
*   your question is lacking reproduction or information
*   the cause of problems is due to poor React or JavaScript skills
*   our [Code of Conduct](https://remotion.dev/coc)
     is violated

Remotion is targeted towards developers and therefore basic skills in React and web development are expected.  
You should also be mindful of the limitations of those technologies as we cannot help you overcome them.

See also[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/support#see-also "Direct link to See also")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [Get help](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/get-help)
    

*   [How to ask for help](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/support#how-to-ask-for-help)
    
*   [Prioritized support](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/support#prioritized-support)
    
*   [Paid consulting](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/support#paid-consulting)
    
*   [When we can't help](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/support#when-we-cant-help)
    
*   [See also](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/support#see-also)

---

# Creating a new project | Remotion | Make videos programmatically

[Skip to main content](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Copy page

On this page

Prerequisites[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/#prerequisites "Direct link to Prerequisites")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

To use Remotion, you need at least [Node](https://nodejs.org/en/download/)
 16 or [Bun](https://bun.sh/)
 1.0.3.

Prompting a video with Claude Code[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/#prompting-a-video-with-claude-code "Direct link to Prompting a video with Claude Code")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

If you plan on prompting videos with **Claude Code**, see: [Prompting videos with Claude Code](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/ai/claude-code)
.

Scaffolding a new project[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/#scaffolding-a-new-project "Direct link to Scaffolding a new project")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

note

🍎 macOS 15 (Sequoia) or later is required. [Older versions are not supported.](https://github.com/remotion-dev/remotion/issues/7027)
  
🐧 [Linux distros need at least version 2.35 of Libc.](https://github.com/remotion-dev/remotion/issues/2439)
  
They also need to [install some additional packages](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/miscellaneous/linux-dependencies)
.  
Alpine Linux and nixOS are unsupported.

*   npm
*   bun
*   pnpm
*   yarn

Use npm as the package manager

`npx create-video@latest`Copy

Use pnpm as the package manager

`pnpm create video`Copy

Use Yarn as the package manager

`yarn create video`Copy

Use Bun as the package manager and runtime

`bun create video`Copy

note

Bun as a runtime is mostly supported. [Read more here](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/bun)
.

Choose the template that is most suitable for you.  
For your first project, we recommend the [Hello World](https://www.remotion.dev/templates/hello-world)
 template.

*   Regular templates
*   Next.js + React Router 7

After the project has been scaffolded, we recommend to open the project in your text editor and starting the [Remotion Studio](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/studio)
:

    npm run dev

After the project has been scaffolded, we recommend to open the project in your text editor and starting the app:

    npm run dev

To start the [Remotion Studio](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/studio)
:

    npm run remotion

Installation in existing projects[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/#installation-in-existing-projects "Direct link to Installation in existing projects")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Want to install Remotion in an existing project? Go here instead: [Installation in existing projects](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/brownfield)

*   [Prerequisites](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/#prerequisites)
    
*   [Prompting a video with Claude Code](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/#prompting-a-video-with-claude-code)
    
*   [Scaffolding a new project](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/#scaffolding-a-new-project)
    
*   [Installation in existing projects](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/#installation-in-existing-projects)

---

# Editor Starter | Remotion | Make videos programmatically

[Skip to main content](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Copy page

On this page

![](https://pub-646d808d9cb240cea53bedc76dd3cd0c.r2.dev/editor-starter-og-image.jpg)  
  

A template containing the most essential elements of a video editor.

*   **Save countless hours** by leveraging our pre-made components for timeline, interactive canvas, font picker, asset uploads, etc.
*   **Get the best of Remotion** put together into one template, culminating years of development and battle-testing.
*   **Customize anything** by adopting a clean, well-structured codebase with 80+ feature flags.

Introduction video[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter#introduction-video "Direct link to Introduction video")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  

Table of contents[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter#table-of-contents "Direct link to Table of contents")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

A demo of the Editor Starter](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/demo)

What to consider before buying](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/before-you-buy)

How to buy the Editor Starter](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/buy)

\
Installation and configuration guide](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/setup)

How to cleanup assets that are no longer needed](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/asset-cleanup)

License[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter#license "Direct link to License")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

 and [its own specific license](https://www.remotion.pro/editor-starter?tab=license)
.

*   Remotion, as an underlying technology of the Remotion Editor Starter, is free to use for individuals and small companies. If you fall into this category, you can buy the Remotion Editor Starter as a one-time purchase. If you are a larger company, you must subscribe to the [Company License](https://www.remotion.pro/license)
    . Read the [Terms & Conditions](https://www.remotion.pro/terms)
    .

     as well as accessible through the dedicated GitHub repository after purchase.

# List of resources | Remotion | Make videos programmatically

[Skip to main content](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/resources#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Copy page

On this page

This list tries to compile all templates, libraries, building blocks and examples in one place. Add more to the list by clicking the "Improve this page" button below!

Templates[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/resources#templates "Direct link to Templates")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Hello World (TypeScript) ([Source code](https://github.com/remotion-dev/template-helloworld)
    , [Preview](https://remotion-helloworld.vercel.app/)
    )
*   Hello World (JavaScript) ([Source code](https://github.com/remotion-dev/template-helloworld-javascript)
    , [Preview](https://template-helloworld-javascript.vercel.app/)
    )
*   Blank ([Source code](https://github.com/remotion-dev/template-empty)
    , [Preview](https://template-empty.vercel.app/)
    )
*   React Three Fiber ([Source code](https://github.com/remotion-dev/template-three)
    , [Preview](https://template-three-remotion.vercel.app/)
    )
*   Stills ([Source code](https://github.com/remotion-dev/template-still)
    , [Preview](https://template-still.vercel.app/)
    )
*   Text-to-Speech Template (Azure) ([Source code](https://github.com/FelippeChemello/Remotion-TTS-Example)
    )
*   Text-to-Speech Template (Google) ([Source code](https://github.com/thecmdrunner/remotion-gtts-template)
    )
*   Audiogram ([Source code](https://github.com/remotion-dev/template-audiogram)
    )
*   Music Visualization ([Source code](https://github.com/remotion-dev/template-music-visualization)
    )
*   Skia ([Source code](https://github.com/remotion-dev/template-skia)
    )
*   [Hello World Template with pull request video previews](https://github.com/stoat-dev/example-remotion)
    
*   [Library starter template with best practices](https://github.com/remotion-dev/library-starter)
    
*   Video Editor & Timeline Component (Next.js, Tailwind CSS) ([Source Code](https://github.com/sambowenhughes/a-react-video-editor)
    , [Preview](https://www.reactvideoeditor.com/open-source?utm_source=remotion)
    )
*   [Free Remotion Components](https://www.clippkit.com/)
    
*   [Remotion Bits - Ready-made blocks to use for you and your agents](https://remotion-bits.dev/)
    
*   Docker Build & Render ([Source code](https://github.com/scotthavird/remotion-docker-template)
    )
*   [Template for deploying Remotion on Railway](https://railway.com/deploy/remotion-on-rails)
     ([Blog](https://medium.com/@viveknathani/remotion-on-rails-0852acc9595e)
    )
*   [Multiple shared Remotion components in a Monorepo template](https://github.com/Takamasa045/remotion-studio-monorepo)

     - Captioning helpers and styles

Full projects[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/resources#full-projects "Direct link to Full projects")

# Before you buy the Editor Starter | Remotion | Make videos programmatically

[Skip to main content](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/before-you-buy#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Copy page

On this page

Is the Editor Starter for you?

### You want to make a video editor[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/before-you-buy#you-want-to-make-a-video-editor "Direct link to You want to make a video editor")

You don't want to edit a video for yourself, you want to make a video editor.  
You are a developer or have a developer on your team.

### You want to build it with React 19[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/before-you-buy#you-want-to-build-it-with-react-19 "Direct link to You want to build it with React 19")

The Editor Starter is built with React 19.  
Your application is on React 19 already or you are willing to start your app from scratch using our template.

### You want to use Remotion[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/before-you-buy#you-want-to-use-remotion "Direct link to You want to use Remotion")

Remotion is used for playback and for rendering videos.  
You are fine with using Remotion and are okay with its [license](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/license)
.

### You want to use Tailwind v4[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/before-you-buy#you-want-to-use-tailwind-v4 "Direct link to You want to use Tailwind v4")

The Editor Starter uses Tailwind v4. To integrate it, your app must also use Tailwind v4.

If you are starting from scratch, our template sets up Tailwind v4 for you. If you are integrating into an existing project that doesn’t use Tailwind v4, plan to add or upgrade to Tailwind v4.

### You want to use TypeScript[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/before-you-buy#you-want-to-use-typescript "Direct link to You want to use TypeScript")

The Editor Starter is built with TypeScript. You are fine with using TypeScript.

### You want to iterate on the editor[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/before-you-buy#you-want-to-iterate-on-the-editor "Direct link to You want to iterate on the editor")

The Editor Starter is only a starter template.  
We assume you will want to fork it, add features or integrate it with AI.

You are capable of doing so and are aware of the [features that are not included in the Editor Starter](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features-not-included)
.

See also[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/before-you-buy#see-also "Direct link to See also")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [Features](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features)
    
*   [Features not included](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features-not-included)
    
*   [Frequently asked questions](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/faq)
    
*   [Dependencies](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/dependencies)
    
*   [Remotion License](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/license)
    

*   [You want to make a video editor](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/before-you-buy#you-want-to-make-a-video-editor)
    
*   [You want to build it with React 19](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/before-you-buy#you-want-to-build-it-with-react-19)
    
*   [You want to use Remotion](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/before-you-buy#you-want-to-use-remotion)
    
*   [You want to use Tailwind v4](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/before-you-buy#you-want-to-use-tailwind-v4)
    
*   [You want to use TypeScript](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/before-you-buy#you-want-to-use-typescript)
    
*   [You want to iterate on the editor](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/before-you-buy#you-want-to-iterate-on-the-editor)
    
*   [See also](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/before-you-buy#see-also)

Copy page

On this page

Try out the Editor Starter yourself!

| Empty project | Announcement video |
| --- | --- |
| ![Empty Editor Starter project](https://pub-646d808d9cb240cea53bedc76dd3cd0c.r2.dev/demo-empty-state.png) | ![Editor Starter announcement video](https://pub-646d808d9cb240cea53bedc76dd3cd0c.r2.dev/demo-editor-starter.png) |
| [](https://editor-starter.remotion.dev/)<br><br>[**Try empty project**](https://editor-starter.remotion.dev/) | [](https://editor-starter.remotion.dev/#)<br><br>[**Edit announcement video**](https://editor-starter.remotion.dev/#) |

Notes[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/demo#notes "Direct link to Notes")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   The announcement video link is a long URL, it might not be sharable everywhere.
*   Don't rely on these to edit serious projects. We might change the internal data structure of videos as we develop the Editor Starter, breaking your projects.
*   There is no responsive design, so the demo might not look good on mobile.

*   [Notes](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/demo#notes)

# Buy the Editor Starter | Remotion | Make videos programmatically

[Skip to main content](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/buy#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Copy page

On this page

To buy the Editor Starter for $600, visit **[remotion.pro/editor-starter](https://www.remotion.pro/editor-starter)
**.

### Upgrading from the Timeline[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/buy#upgrading-from-the-timeline "Direct link to Upgrading from the Timeline")

If you have already bought the [Timeline](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/timeline)
 and are buying the Editor Starter, you are eligible for a full refund on the Timeline component.  
Buy from the same email address and then contact us at [\[email protected\]](https://www.remotion.dev/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#f19998b183949c9e85989e9fdf959487)
 from the email address you used to buy the Timeline.

### For Enterprise License holders[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/buy#for-enterprise-license-holders "Direct link to For Enterprise License holders")

The Editor Starter is included in the Enterprise License.  
Provide a list of GitHub users you would like to invite in our private channel with us to get access to the Editor Starter.

*   [Upgrading from the Timeline](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/buy#upgrading-from-the-timeline)
    
*   [For Enterprise License holders](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/buy#for-enterprise-license-holders)

---

# Remotion Recorder | Remotion | Make videos programmatically

[Skip to main content](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/recorder#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Copy page

On this page

![Animated Remotion Recorder Logo](https://www.remotion.dev/recorder/recorder-logo-light.gif)

The Remotion Recorder is a video production tool **built entirely in JavaScript**.

Recording[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/recorder#recording "Direct link to Recording")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Record your facecam and screen in sync but independently. This allows you to create a dynamic layout later.

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| ![](https://www.remotion.dev/recorder/individual.png) |     |
| Record webcam and display as separate streams. Up to 4 sources can be recorded at the same time! | Ensure you are in the center of the video and you are not too close to the microphone. |

Different platforms require different formats[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/recorder#different-platforms-require-different-formats "Direct link to Different platforms require different formats")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Each platform has its own conventions for how videos should look.

|     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- |
| ![](https://www.remotion.dev/recorder/vps-square.png) | ![](https://www.remotion.dev/recorder/vps-landscape.png) | WIP |
| **Twitter, LinkedIn**<br><br>1:1<br><br>Muted by default<br><br>Captions burned in | **YouTube**<br><br>16:9<br><br>Audio by default<br><br>Captions as .srt file<br><br>Endcard with related videos | **<br><br>TikTok, Reels, Shorts<br><br>[planned](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/recorder#tiktok-format)<br>**<br><br>9:16<br><br>Safe space at bottom required<br><br>Word-by-word captions |

The goal of the Recorder: Edit once **and feel native on each platform**.

This has a measurable impact on the performance of your post: For example, a 1:1 video on X [results in 30-35% higher video views and a 80-100% higher engagement rate](https://buffer.com/resources/square-video-vs-landscape-video/)
.

Captions are essential[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/recorder#captions-are-essential "Direct link to Captions are essential")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Generate captions locally using [Whisper.cpp](https://github.com/ggerganov/whisper.cpp)
. The Recorder will automatically install it for you.  
AI will make mistakes - so we make it easy to correct them.

|     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Captions have word-level timings | Orphan words are avoided by balancing the text layout | Use `backticks` to highlight technical terms |

|     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Fix captions quickly by clicking on a word | ![](https://www.remotion.dev/recorder/autocorrect.png)  <br><br>Write a JavaScript function to fix common misspellings | ![](https://www.remotion.dev/recorder/asjson.png)  <br><br>Captions are stored as editable JSON |

Agnostic endcards[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/recorder#agnostic-endcards "Direct link to Agnostic endcards")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Your viewer watched the entire video! Now don't make them forget about you.

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| ![](https://www.remotion.dev/recorder/endcard-square.png)<br><br>If you post on Twitter, the call-to-action is "Follow" and your other platforms are linked. | ![](https://www.remotion.dev/recorder/endcard-youtube.png)  <br><br>If you post on YouTube, the call to action is "Subscribe" and cutouts for related videos are made. |

The endcard is written with React and CSS, so can be easily customized.

Customization[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/recorder#customization "Direct link to Customization")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Having access to the entire source code, you can use CSS to customize the look of the Recorder.

|     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- |
| ![](https://www.remotion.dev/recorder/theme-light.png)<br><br>Look of the built-in light theme | ![](https://www.remotion.dev/recorder/theme-dark.png)  <br><br>Look of the built-in dark theme | ![](https://www.remotion.dev/recorder/theme-fancy.png)  <br><br>Use CSS to make it your own! |

The Recorder is built with TypeScript and React, and you can fully customize it's behavior. It's a hackable video editor.

Silence removal[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/recorder#silence-removal "Direct link to Silence removal")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Automatically remove the silence at the start and end of your take.

Music[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/recorder#music "Direct link to Music")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Add background music to your video and automatically fade between the different sections.  
Three exclusive tracks are included that you may use for your video.

Layouting[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/recorder#layouting "Direct link to Layouting")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Whether you only record your facecam, or also your display, the Recorder always finds the ideal layout - even if you mix landscape and portrait videos.

Transitions[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/recorder#transitions "Direct link to Transitions")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Enable clean transitions between scenes - no matter if you change the layout during the video.

Chapters[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/recorder#chapters "Direct link to Chapters")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Mark a scene as the beginning of a new chapter and automatically generate animated chapters.

B-Roll[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/recorder#b-roll "Direct link to B-Roll")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Add images and videos on top of your facecam or display while your voiceover is playing to give the video more depth.

Exporting[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/recorder#exporting "Direct link to Exporting")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Export your video with much control over the encoding options.  
Render a video with a single click on a button, the CLI, Node.JS APIs, GitHub Actions, or even on AWS Lambda!

See also[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/recorder#see-also "Direct link to See also")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [Editor Starter](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter)
    

* * *

1) 9:16 format is not yet implemented.

*   [Recording](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/recorder#recording)
    
*   [Different platforms require different formats](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/recorder#different-platforms-require-different-formats)
    
*   [Captions are essential](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/recorder#captions-are-essential)
    
*   [Agnostic endcards](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/recorder#agnostic-endcards)
    
*   [Customization](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/recorder#customization)
    
*   [Silence removal](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/recorder#silence-removal)
    
*   [Music](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/recorder#music)
    
*   [Layouting](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/recorder#layouting)
    
*   [Transitions](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/recorder#transitions)
    
*   [Chapters](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/recorder#chapters)
    
*   [B-Roll](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/recorder#b-roll)

# Timeline – Setup | Remotion | Make videos programmatically

[Skip to main content](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/timeline/setup#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Copy page

On this page

Installation[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/timeline/setup#installation "Direct link to Installation")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  Copy the `timeline/` folder to your application
2.  Install the required dependencies:

    npm install remotion @remotion/player @remotion/media-utils tailwindcss

Tailwind Configuration[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/timeline/setup#tailwind-configuration "Direct link to Tailwind Configuration")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Using Tailwind CSS v4 (Current Project)[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/timeline/setup#using-tailwind-css-v4-current-project "Direct link to Using Tailwind CSS v4 (Current Project)")

This project uses Tailwind CSS V4. Import `src/timeline/theme/timeline.css` in your global CSS file:

    @import './timeline/theme/timeline.css';

You can customize the colors by modifying the values in the `@theme` block.

### Using Tailwind CSS v3 (Legacy)[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/timeline/setup#using-tailwind-css-v3-legacy "Direct link to Using Tailwind CSS v3 (Legacy)")

Instructions for Tailwind v3

Use `src/timeline/theme/timeline-preset.mjs` as a preset in your `tailwind.config.js`:

Then, set up your `tailwind.config.js` like this:

    import timelinePreset from './timeline/theme/timeline-preset.mjs';
    
    /** @type {import('tailwindcss').Config} */
    export default {
      presets: [timelinePreset],
      content: ['./index.html', './src/**/*.{js,ts,jsx,tsx}'],
      theme: {
        extend: {
          // Add your custom theme extensions here
        },
      },
    };

*   [Installation](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/timeline/setup#installation)
    
*   [Tailwind Configuration](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/timeline/setup#tailwind-configuration)
    *   [Using Tailwind CSS v4 (Current Project)](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/timeline/setup#using-tailwind-css-v4-current-project)
        
    *   [Using Tailwind CSS v3 (Legacy)](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/timeline/setup#using-tailwind-css-v3-legacy)

---

# Setup | Remotion | Make videos programmatically

[Skip to main content](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/setup#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Copy page

On this page

After obtaining the Editor Starter, you should [fork](https://github.com/remotion-dev/editor-starter/fork)
 it to your own account.

Once you have forked the repository, you can clone it to your local machine, and install the dependencies by doing the following:

Start a new project[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/setup#start-a-new-project "Direct link to Start a new project")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

If you are starting a new project, you can stay in your repository and install dependencies:

    npm i

Run the app using:

    npm run dev

The app may be deployed like any other React Router 7 app, for example by connecting the repository to [Vercel](https://vercel.com/)
.

Copying into an existing project[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/setup#copying-into-an-existing-project "Direct link to Copying into an existing project")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

If you already have a project, you can copy the `src/editor` folder into your existing project.  
The following instructions were tested on a new Next.js project with Tailwind and TypeScript enabled, but it should work with any React framework.

[](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/setup#1)

[1](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/setup#1)

[](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/setup#1)
Install the [dependencies](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/dependencies)
.

[](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/setup#2)

[2](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/setup#2)

[](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/setup#2)
Copy the `src/editor` folder into your existing project.

[](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/setup#3)

[3](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/setup#3)

[](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/setup#3)
Mount the editor in your app. For example, in Next.js, in `page/app.tsx`:

page/app.tsx

`import { Editor } from "./editor/editor"; export default function Home() {   return <Editor />; }`Copy

[](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/setup#4)

[4](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/setup#4)

[](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/setup#4)
Set up the [backend routes](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/backend-routes)
.

Setting up asset uploads[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/setup#setting-up-asset-uploads "Direct link to Setting up asset uploads")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

If the user drops in any assets, you can configure them to be uploaded to an S3 bucket.  
See [Asset uploads](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/asset-uploads)
 for more information.

Setting up rendering[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/setup#setting-up-rendering "Direct link to Setting up rendering")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

See [Rendering](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/rendering)
 for how to setup the Remotion Lambda integration.

Setting up captions[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/setup#setting-up-captions "Direct link to Setting up captions")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can setup AI transcription for audio and video assets. See [Captioning](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/captioning)
 on how to set it up.

*   [Start a new project](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/setup#start-a-new-project)
    
*   [Copying into an existing project](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/setup#copying-into-an-existing-project)
    
*   [Setting up asset uploads](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/setup#setting-up-asset-uploads)
    
*   [Setting up rendering](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/setup#setting-up-rendering)
    
*   [Setting up captions](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/setup#setting-up-captions)

# Timeline – Usage | Remotion | Make videos programmatically

[Skip to main content](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/timeline/usage#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Copy page

On this page

Component Integration[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/timeline/usage#component-integration "Direct link to Component Integration")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Import and use the timeline components in your React application.

For optimal performance and proper functionality, structure your components like this:

    import type {PlayerRef} from '@remotion/player';
    import {Timeline, TimelineContainer} from './timeline/remotion-timeline/components/timeline';
    import {TimelineProvider} from './timeline/remotion-timeline/context/provider';
    import {TimelineSizeProvider} from './timeline/remotion-timeline/context/timeline-size-provider';
    import {TimelineZoomProvider} from './timeline/remotion-timeline/context/timeline-zoom-provider';
    import {PreviewContainer} from './layout';
    
    export const App = () => {
      const playerRef = useRef<PlayerRef>(null);
      const timelineContainerRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);
      const timelineContainerSize = useElementSize(timelineContainerRef);
      const timelineContainerWidth = timelineContainerSize?.width;
    
      return (
        <TimelineProvider
          onChange={(newState) => {
            console.log('New timeline state:', newState);
          }}
          initialState={initialState}
        >
          <TimelineZoomProvider initialZoom={1}>
            <PreviewContainer>
              <VideoPreview loop playerRef={playerRef} />
              <ActionRow playerRef={playerRef} />
            </PreviewContainer>
    
            <TimelineContainer timelineContainerRef={timelineContainerRef}>
              {timelineContainerWidth ? (
                <TimelineSizeProvider containerWidth={timelineContainerWidth}>
                  <Timeline playerRef={playerRef} />
                </TimelineSizeProvider>
              ) : null}
            </TimelineContainer>
          </TimelineZoomProvider>
        </TimelineProvider>
      );
    };

This structure ensures:

*   Timeline state management through `TimelineProvider`
*   Zoom functionality with `TimelineZoomProvider`
*   Proper sizing calculations with `TimelineSizeProvider`
*   Responsive timeline container with `TimelineContainer`

Default state structure[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/timeline/usage#default-state-structure "Direct link to Default state structure")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The project includes a predefined state structure for timeline tracks and items. You can check [TimelineProvider component](https://github.com/remotion-dev/timeline/blob/main/src/timeline/remotion-timeline/context/provider.tsx)
 for the state structure.

Video Preview[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/timeline/usage#video-preview "Direct link to Video Preview")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This project includes a `CanvasComposition`, a Remotion composition that renders the timeline state in the Remotion Player.

You can:

*   Use the provided composition as-is for quick prototyping
*   Adapt it to your needs
*   Replace it with your own composition while keeping the timeline functionality

Check `video-preview.tsx` and `App.tsx` for implementation examples.

State Persistence[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/timeline/usage#state-persistence "Direct link to State Persistence")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The `TimelineProvider` includes an `onChange` callback that fires whenever the timeline state changes (adding/removing tracks, moving items, etc.). Use this to save the editor state to your backend:

    function VideoEditor() {
      const saveToServer = async (state: TimelineState) => {
        try {
          await fetch('/api/save-timeline', {
            method: 'POST',
            body: JSON.stringify(state),
          });
        } catch (error) {
          console.error('Failed to save timeline state:', error);
        }
      };
    
      return (
        <TimelineProvider onChange={saveToServer} initialState={initialState}>
          <div className="video-editor">
            <VideoPreview playerRef={playerRef} />
            <ActionRow playerRef={playerRef} />
            <TimelineRoot playerRef={playerRef} />
          </div>
        </TimelineProvider>
      );
    }

Example Project Structure[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/timeline/usage#example-project-structure "Direct link to Example Project Structure")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

In `App.tsx`, you can see the complete implementation of the timeline component.

The example project demonstrates:

*   Importing the timeline components
*   Using the `ActionRow` component to use timeline zoom and add clips
*   Using the `TimelineRoot` component to render the timeline

*   [Component Integration](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/timeline/usage#component-integration)
    
*   [Default state structure](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/timeline/usage#default-state-structure)
    
*   [Video Preview](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/timeline/usage#video-preview)
    
*   [State Persistence](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/timeline/usage#state-persistence)
    
*   [Example Project Structure](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/timeline/usage#example-project-structure)

---

# @remotion/animated-emoji | Remotion | Make videos programmatically

[Skip to main content](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/animated-emoji/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Copy page

On this page

_available from v4.0.187_

This package wraps the [Google Fonts Animated Emoji](https://googlefonts.github.io/noto-emoji-animation/)
 into Remotion components.

Installation[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/animated-emoji/#installation "Direct link to Installation")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Remotion CLI
*   npm
*   bun
*   pnpm
*   yarn

npx remotion add @remotion/animated-emoji

Copy

npm i --save-exact @remotion/[\[email protected\]](https://www.remotion.dev/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection)

Copy

This assumes you are currently using v4.0.448 of Remotion.  
Also update `remotion` and all `` `@remotion/*` `` packages to the same version.  
Remove all `^` character in front of the version numbers of it as it can lead to a version conflict.

pnpm i @remotion/[\[email protected\]](https://www.remotion.dev/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection)

Copy

This assumes you are currently using v4.0.448 of Remotion.  
Also update `remotion` and all `` `@remotion/*` `` packages to the same version.  
Remove all `^` character in front of the version numbers of it as it can lead to a version conflict.

bun i @remotion/[\[email protected\]](https://www.remotion.dev/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection)

Copy

This assumes you are currently using v4.0.448 of Remotion.  
Also update `remotion` and all `` `@remotion/*` `` packages to the same version.  
Remove all `^` character in front of the version numbers of it as it can lead to a version conflict.

yarn --exact add @remotion/[\[email protected\]](https://www.remotion.dev/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection)

Copy

This assumes you are currently using v4.0.448 of Remotion.  
Also update `remotion` and all `` `@remotion/*` `` packages to the same version.  
Remove all `^` character in front of the version numbers of it as it can lead to a version conflict.

APIs[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/animated-emoji/#apis "Direct link to APIs")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The following APIs are available:

[**<AnimatedEmoji>**\
\
Component for rendering an animated emoji.](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/animated-emoji/animated-emoji)
[**getAvailableEmoji()**\
\
Get a list of available emoji.](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/animated-emoji/get-available-emoji)

License[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/animated-emoji/#license "Direct link to License")

.

# Parameterized videos | Remotion | Make videos programmatically

[Skip to main content](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/parameterized-rendering#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Copy page

On this page

Remotion allows for [ingesting](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/passing-props)
, [validating](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/schemas)
, [visually editing](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/visual-editing)
, and transforming data that may be used to parametrize a video.

Data may [influence the content](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/data-fetching)
 of the video, or the [metadata](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/dynamic-metadata)
 such as width, height, duration or framerate.

High-level overview[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/parameterized-rendering#high-level-overview "Direct link to High-level overview")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Remotion allows the passing of [props](https://react.dev/learn/passing-props-to-a-component)
 to a React component.  
Props are a React concept and take the shape of a JavaScript object.

To determine the data which gets passed to the video, the following steps are taken:

[](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/parameterized-rendering#1)

[1](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/parameterized-rendering#1)

[](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/parameterized-rendering#1)
**Default props** are defined statically, so that the video can be designed in the Studio without any data.  

*   The default props define the shape of the data.
    
*   A schema can be defined and validated.
    
*   In absence of data, default props can be edited in the Remotion Studio.
    

[](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/parameterized-rendering#2)

[2](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/parameterized-rendering#2)

[](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/parameterized-rendering#2)
**Input props** may be specified when rendering a video to override the default props.  

*   Input props will be merged together with default props, where input props have priority.
    

[](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/parameterized-rendering#3)

[3](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/parameterized-rendering#3)

[](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/parameterized-rendering#3)
**Using [`calculateMetadata()`](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/data-fetching)
**, postprocessing of the props may be performed and metadata be dynamically calculated.  

*   For example, given a URL is passed as a prop, it may be fetched and the content added to the props.
    
*   Asynchronous calculation of the video duration and other metadata is also possible here.
    

[](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/parameterized-rendering#4)

[4](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/parameterized-rendering#4)

[](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/parameterized-rendering#4)
**The final props** are passed to the React component.

*   The component may dynamically render content based on the props.
    

See [here](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/props-resolution)
 for a visual explanation and more details of how the resolution process works.

Table of contents[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/parameterized-rendering#table-of-contents "Direct link to Table of contents")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [Passing props](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/passing-props)
    
*   [Defining a Schema](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/schemas)
    
*   [Visual editing](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/visual-editing)
    
*   [Data fetching](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/data-fetching)
    
*   [Variable metadata](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/dynamic-metadata)
    
*   [How props get resolved](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/props-resolution)
    

See also[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/parameterized-rendering#see-also "Direct link to See also")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can use the [Remotion Player](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/player)
 to display a Remotion component in a React app and dynamically change the content without rendering the video, to create experiences where the content updates in real-time.

*   [High-level overview](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/parameterized-rendering#high-level-overview)
    
*   [Table of contents](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/parameterized-rendering#table-of-contents)
    
*   [See also](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/parameterized-rendering#see-also)

---

# @remotion/bundler | Remotion | Make videos programmatically

[Skip to main content](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/bundler#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Copy page

On this page

A package containing the [`bundle()`](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/bundle)
 function, which takes a Remotion project and bundles it using Webpack, preparing it to be used by server-side rendering functions such as [`getCompositions()`](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/renderer/get-compositions)
 and [`renderMedia()`](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/renderer/render-media)

Also update `remotion` and all `` `@remotion/*` `` packages to the same version.  
Remove all `^` character in front of the version numbers of it as it can lead to a version conflict.

Also update `remotion` and all `` `@remotion/*` `` packages to the same version.

Also update `remotion` and all `` `@remotion/*` `` packages to the same version.  
Remove all `^` character in front of the version numbers of it as it can lead to a version conflict.

yarn --exact add @remotion/[\[email protected\]](https://www.remotion.dev/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection)

# Dependencies of the Editor Starter | Remotion | Make videos programmatically

[Skip to main content](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/dependencies#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Copy page

On this page

The Editor Starter is built in [React 19](https://react.dev/)
 and [Remotion](https://www.remotion.dev/)
.  
The reference implementation is built in [React Router v7](https://reactrouter.com/)
 with the goal of being easily portable to other frameworks.  
[Tailwind CSS v4](https://tailwindcss.com/)
 is used for styling.

Captions[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/dependencies#captions "Direct link to Captions")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

OpenAI's [Audio API](https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/speech-to-text)
 is used for captioning.  
Captioning is optional, but it is enabled by default and an OpenAI API key is required to use it.

Asset uploads[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/dependencies#asset-uploads "Direct link to Asset uploads")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Assets are uploaded by default to AWS S3](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/asset-uploads)
.  
This means that in order for asset uploads to work, an AWS account is required and AWS S3 needs to be set up.

Rendering[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/dependencies#rendering "Direct link to Rendering")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Remotion Lambda](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/lambda)
 is used for rendering.  
This means that in order for rendering to work, an AWS account is required and AWS Lambda needs to be set up.

Google Fonts[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/dependencies#google-fonts "Direct link to Google Fonts")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Fonts are loaded from [Google Fonts](https://fonts.google.com/)
. No API key is required for this.

NPM packages[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/dependencies#npm-packages "Direct link to NPM packages")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The following NPM packages are used in the Editor Starter:

    {
      "@aws-sdk/s3-request-presigner": "^3.936.0",
      "@radix-ui/react-context-menu": "^2.2.15",
      "@radix-ui/react-popover": "^1.1.14",
      "@radix-ui/react-select": "^2.2.5",
      "sonner": "^2.0.7",
      "@remotion/captions": "^4.0.331",
      "@remotion/cli": "^4.0.331",
      "@remotion/gif": "^4.0.331",
      "@remotion/google-fonts": "^4.0.331",
      "@remotion/lambda": "^4.0.331",
      "@remotion/layout-utils": "^4.0.331",
      "@remotion/media-parser": "^4.0.331",
      "@remotion/openai-whisper": "^4.0.331",
      "@remotion/player": "^4.0.331",
      "@remotion/shapes": "^4.0.331",
      "@tanstack/react-virtual": "^3.13.10",
      "remotion": "^4.0.331",
      "openai": "^4.67.3",
      "zod": "^3.24.3"
    }

It's always recommended to use the latest version of Remotion packages. Currently it is: [`4.0.448`](https://remotion.dev/changelog)
.

Run `npx remotion upgrade` to upgrade to the latest version.

*   [Captions](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/dependencies#captions)
    
*   [Asset uploads](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/dependencies#asset-uploads)
    
*   [Rendering](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/dependencies#rendering)
    
*   [Google Fonts](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/dependencies#google-fonts)
    
*   [NPM packages](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/dependencies#npm-packages)

---

# Embedding videos into Remotion | Remotion | Make videos programmatically

[Skip to main content](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/videos/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Copy page

On this page

You can embed existing videos into Remotion by using the [`<OffthreadVideo>`](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/offthreadvideo)
 component.

    import React from 'react';
    import {OffthreadVideo, staticFile} from 'remotion';
    
    export const MyComp: React.FC = () => {
      return <OffthreadVideo src="https://remotion.media/BigBuckBunny.mp4" />;
    };

Using a local file[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/videos/#using-a-local-file "Direct link to Using a local file")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Put a file into the [`public` folder](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/terminology/public-dir)
 and reference it using [`staticFile()`](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/staticfile)
.

    import React from 'react';
    import {OffthreadVideo, staticFile} from 'remotion';
    
    export const MyComp: React.FC = () => {
      return <OffthreadVideo src={staticFile('video.mp4')} />;
    };

Trimming[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/videos/#trimming "Direct link to Trimming")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

By using the [`trimBefore`](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/offthreadvideo#trimbefore)
 prop, you can remove the first few seconds of the video.  
In the example below, the first two seconds of the video are skipped (assuming a composition FPS of 30).

    import React from 'react';
    import {OffthreadVideo, staticFile} from 'remotion';
    
    export const MyComp: React.FC = () => {
      return <OffthreadVideo src={staticFile('video.mp4')} trimBefore={60} />;
    };

Similarly, you can use [`trimAfter`](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/offthreadvideo#trimafter)
 to trim the end of the video.

    import React from 'react';
    import {OffthreadVideo, staticFile} from 'remotion';
    
    export const MyComp: React.FC = () => {
      return <OffthreadVideo src={staticFile('video.mp4')} trimBefore={60} trimAfter={120} />;
    };

Delaying[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/videos/#delaying "Direct link to Delaying")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Use the [`<Sequence>`](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/sequence)
 component to delay the appearance of a video.  
In the example below, the video will start playing at frame 60.

    import React from 'react';
    import {OffthreadVideo, staticFile, Sequence} from 'remotion';
    
    export const MyComp: React.FC = () => {
      return (
        <Sequence from={60}>
          <OffthreadVideo src={staticFile('video.mp4')} />
        </Sequence>
      );
    };

Size and Position[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/videos/#size-and-position "Direct link to Size and Position")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can size and position the video using CSS.  
You'll find the properties `width`, `height`, `position`, `left`, `top`, `right`, `bottom`, `margin`, `aspectRatio` and `objectFit` useful.

    import React from 'react';
    import {OffthreadVideo, staticFile} from 'remotion';
    
    export const MyComp: React.FC = () => {
      return (
        <OffthreadVideo
          src={staticFile('video.mp4')}
          style={{
            width: 640,
            height: 360,
            position: 'absolute',
            top: 100,
            left: 100,
          }}
        />
      );
    };

Volume[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/videos/#volume "Direct link to Volume")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can set the volume of the video using the [`volume`](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/offthreadvideo#volume)
 prop.

    import React from 'react';
    import {OffthreadVideo, staticFile} from 'remotion';
    
    export const MyComp: React.FC = () => {
      return <OffthreadVideo src={staticFile('video.mp4')} volume={0.5} />;
    };

You can also mute a video using the [`muted`](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/offthreadvideo#muted)
 prop.

    import React from 'react';
    import {OffthreadVideo, staticFile} from 'remotion';
    
    export const MyComp: React.FC = () => {
      return <OffthreadVideo src={staticFile('video.mp4')} muted />;
    };

See [Using Audio](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/audio/volume)
 for more ways you can control volume.

Speed[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/videos/#speed "Direct link to Speed")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can use the [`playbackRate`](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/offthreadvideo#playbackrate)
 prop to change the speed of the video.

    import React from 'react';
    import {OffthreadVideo, staticFile} from 'remotion';
    
    export const MyComp: React.FC = () => {
      return <OffthreadVideo src={staticFile('video.mp4')} playbackRate={2} />;
    };

This only works if the speed is constant. See also: [Changing the speed of a video over time](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/miscellaneous/snippets/accelerated-video)
.

Looping[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/videos/#looping "Direct link to Looping")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

See: [Looping an `<OffthreadVideo>`](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/offthreadvideo#looping-a-offthreadvideo)

GIFs[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/videos/#gifs "Direct link to GIFs")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------

See: [Using GIFs](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/gif)

See also[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/videos/#see-also "Direct link to See also")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [`<OffthreadVideo>`](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/offthreadvideo)
    
*   [Using Audio](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/using-audio)
    

*   [Using a local file](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/videos/#using-a-local-file)
    
*   [Trimming](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/videos/#trimming)
    
*   [Delaying](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/videos/#delaying)
    
*   [Size and Position](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/videos/#size-and-position)
    
*   [Volume](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/videos/#volume)
    
*   [Speed](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/videos/#speed)
    
*   [Looping](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/videos/#looping)
    
*   [GIFs](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/videos/#gifs)
    
*   [See also](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/videos/#see-also)

---

# Using audio | Remotion | Make videos programmatically

[Skip to main content](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/using-audio#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Copy page

On this page

Remotion provides powerful capabilities for working with audio in your compositions.

[**Importing audio**\
\
Add audio to your compositions](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/audio/importing)
[**Delaying audio**\
\
Delay the start time of audio elements](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/audio/delaying)
[**Creating audio from video**\
\
Use audio from videos](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/audio/from-video)
[**Controlling volume**\
\
Control the volume of audio elements](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/audio/volume)
[**Muting audio**\
\
Mute audio elements](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/audio/muting)
[**Controlling speed**\
\
Change the speed of audio elements](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/audio/speed)
[**Controlling pitch**\
\
Change the pitch of audio elements](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/audio/pitch)
[**Visualizing audio**\
\
Visualize audio elements](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/audio/visualization)
[**Exporting audio**\
\
Export audio](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/audio/exporting)
[**Order of operations**\
\
Control the order of operations for audio elements](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/audio/order-of-operations)

See also[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/using-audio#see-also "Direct link to See also")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [Importing assets](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/assets)
    
*   [Audio visualization](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/audio/visualization)
    
*   [`<Html5Audio />`](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/html5-audio)
     tag

*   [See also](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/using-audio#see-also)
