# Features in the Editor Starter | Remotion | Make videos programmatically

[Skip to main content](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Copy page

On this page

On this page, we list the features that are available in the [Editor Starter](https://remotion.pro/editor-starter)
.

The Editor Starter consists of the following parts illustrated in this graphic:

![](https://pub-646d808d9cb240cea53bedc76dd3cd0c.r2.dev/Remotion%20Editor%20Starter%20Structure.png)

Feature flags[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#feature-flags "Direct link to Feature flags")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Many features are behind feature flags, which can be enabled or disabled by switching the flags in [`src/editor/flags.ts`](https://github.com/remotion-dev/editor-starter/blob/main/src/editor/flags.ts)
.

Feature flags are useful:

*   To disable features you don't need
*   To search the codebase for them and find where a feature is implemented
*   To understand which features you are adopting for your own project

On this page; if a feature has a flag, you can click on it to see the usages in the codebase. The links for the feature flags will only work if you have bought the Editor Starter (i.e., you have access to the repository) and are logged into GitHub.

Item types[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#item-types "Direct link to Item types")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

We support the following item types by default:

*   Images
*   Videos
*   Audios
*   GIFs
*   Text
*   Solid
*   Captions

To learn more about them and how to add a new item type, see: [Tracks, items and assets](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/tracks-items-assets)

Action row[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#action-row "Direct link to Action row")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The action row is the top toolbar.

### Draw solid tool[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#draw-solid-tool "Direct link to Draw solid tool")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_DRAW_SOLID_TOOL`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_DRAW_SOLID_TOOL&type=code)

Shows a button to draw a solid item onto the canvas.

### Add text tool[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#add-text-tool "Direct link to Add text tool")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_CREATE_TEXT_TOOL`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_CREATE_TEXT_TOOL&type=code)

Shows a button to add a text item onto the canvas.

### Import assets tool[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#import-assets-tool "Direct link to Import assets tool")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_IMPORT_ASSETS_TOOL`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_IMPORT_ASSETS_TOOL&type=code)

Shows a button to import assets onto the canvas.

### Undo button[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#undo-button "Direct link to Undo button")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_UNDO_BUTTON`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_UNDO_BUTTON&type=code)

Shows a button to undo the last action.

### Redo button[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#redo-button "Direct link to Redo button")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_REDO_BUTTON`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_REDO_BUTTON&type=code)

Shows a button to redo the last action.

### Save button[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#save-button "Direct link to Save button")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_SAVE_BUTTON`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_SAVE_BUTTON&type=code)

Shows a button to save the current state of the project.

### Download state button[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#download-state-button "Direct link to Download state button")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_DOWNLOAD_STATE`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_DOWNLOAD_STATE&type=code)

Shows a button to download the current state of the project.

### Load state button[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#load-state-button "Direct link to Load state button")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_LOAD_STATE`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_LOAD_STATE&type=code)

Shows a button to load a state of the project that has been downloaded with the [`FEATURE_DOWNLOAD_STATE`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_DOWNLOAD_STATE&type=code)
 button.

Timeline[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#timeline "Direct link to Timeline")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The timeline allows you to see a overview of the content and to scrub through the video.  
Items are organized in "tracks", allowing multiple items to be placed at the same time and to allow for controlling how they overlap.

### Draggable playhead[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#draggable-playhead "Direct link to Draggable playhead")

Pressing and dragging on the time ticks allows you to move the playhead and therefore the position of the video.  
If you drag the playhead to the edge of the timeline, the timeline will scroll.

### Dropping assets[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#dropping-assets "Direct link to Dropping assets")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_DROP_ASSETS_ON_TIMELINE`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_DROP_ASSETS_ON_TIMELINE&type=code)

An asset (image, video, audio, GIF) can be dropped on the timeline to import it and add a new layer.  
The item type is detected by [Remotion Media Parser](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/media-parser/foreign-file-types)
 and an appropriate item is created.

### Filmstrip thumbnails[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#filmstrip-thumbnails "Direct link to Filmstrip thumbnails")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_FILMSTRIP`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_FILMSTRIP&type=code)

For video items, preview images are displayed on the timeline.

### Automatic duration[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#automatic-duration "Direct link to Automatic duration")

When assets are imported, items are automatically assigned appropriate durations based on the asset's length. Video and audio items use the media duration, while images and text use default durations.

### Drag and dropping[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#drag-and-dropping "Direct link to Drag and dropping")

Items can be dragged and repositioned on the timeline. Multi-select is supported, allowing you to drag multiple items at once. A shadow overlay shows where the moving item is being placed when dragging.

### Extend handles[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#extend-handles "Direct link to Extend handles")

You can drag on either side of an item to extend or shrink the item.

### Max trim indicators[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#max-trim-indicators "Direct link to Max trim indicators")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_MAX_TRIM_INDICATORS`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_MAX_TRIM_INDICATORS&type=code)

When extending an item, the maximum trim position is shown with a white outline of the selected item.

### Audio waveform[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#audio-waveform "Direct link to Audio waveform")

Feature flags: [`FEATURE_WAVEFORM`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_WAVEFORM&type=code)
 and [`FEATURE_AUDIO_WAVEFORM_FOR_VIDEO_ITEM`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_AUDIO_WAVEFORM_FOR_VIDEO_ITEM&type=code)

Video and audio items display a waveform at the bottom of the timeline.

### Volume control[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#volume-control "Direct link to Volume control")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_TIMELINE_VOLUME_CONTROL`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_TIMELINE_VOLUME_CONTROL&type=code)

Audio and video items display a draggable volume line on the timeline waveform. Dragging vertically adjusts the volume in decibels with a visual indicator showing the current level.

### Fade in and out[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#fade-in-and-out "Direct link to Fade in and out")

Feature flags: [`FEATURE_AUDIO_FADE_CONTROL`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_AUDIO_FADE_CONTROL&type=code)
 and [`FEATURE_VISUAL_FADE_CONTROL`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_VISUAL_FADE_CONTROL&type=code)

Items support opacity fade in and fade out effects. Audio and video items support fades as well. Draggable handles appear on hover at the start and end of items to adjust fade duration, with visual curves showing the fade effect.

### Splitting items[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#splitting-items "Direct link to Splitting items")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_SPLIT_ITEM`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_SPLIT_ITEM&type=code)

Items can be split at the current playhead position using the scissors tool.  
The split creates two separate items, properly handling fade effects and media start times for the new segments.

### Multi-select / Marquee selection[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#multi-select--marquee-selection "Direct link to Multi-select / Marquee selection")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_TIMELINE_MARQUEE_SELECTION`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_TIMELINE_MARQUEE_SELECTION&type=code)

Multiple items can be selected using Cmd/Ctrl+click (alternatively Shift+click) to add items to the selection, or so called Marquee selection to create a selection rectangle that selects all items within its bounds. Selected items can be moved, deleted, or edited together.

### Hide track[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#hide-track "Direct link to Hide track")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_HIDE_TRACKS`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_HIDE_TRACKS&type=code)

Allows hiding a specific track on the timeline.

### Mute track[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#mute-track "Direct link to Mute track")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_MUTE_TRACKS`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_MUTE_TRACKS&type=code)

Allows muting a specific track on the timeline.

### Rolling edits[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#rolling-edits "Direct link to Rolling edits")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_ROLLING_EDITS`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_ROLLING_EDITS&type=code)

Rolling edits are a video editing feature that allows you to adjust the cut point between two adjacent timeline items without changing their total combined duration or position on the timeline.

### Follow playhead while playing[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#follow-playhead-while-playing "Direct link to Follow playhead while playing")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_FOLLOW_PLAYHEAD_WHILE_PLAYING`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_FOLLOW_PLAYHEAD_WHILE_PLAYING&type=code)

Scrolls the timeline as the playhead moves forward.

### Timeline Snapping[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#timeline-snapping "Direct link to Timeline Snapping")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_TIMELINE_SNAPPING`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_TIMELINE_SNAPPING&type=code)

Enables snapping on the timeline, allowing items to align with other items, the playhead, or grid positions when dragged. This simplifies positioning and transitions. Activate snapping by clicking the magnet icon.

Inspector[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#inspector "Direct link to Inspector")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

On the right side of the screen, an inspector is displayed which allows for editing the properties of the selected item.  
The following features are available.

### Composition inspector[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#composition-inspector "Direct link to Composition inspector")

If no item is selected, controls for the whole composition are displayed (i.e., composition inspector):

*   Setting the dimensions
*   Triggering a render

### Swap dimensions button[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#swap-dimensions-button "Direct link to Swap dimensions button")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_SWAP_COMPOSITION_DIMENSIONS_BUTTON`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_SWAP_COMPOSITION_DIMENSIONS_BUTTON&type=code)

Shows a button to swap the width and height of the composition (i.e., within the composition inspector).

### Source info[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#source-info "Direct link to Source info")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_SOURCE_CONTROL`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_SOURCE_CONTROLO&type=code)

Shows information about the associated [asset](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/tracks-items-assets#assets)
 of a selected item, such as the file name, file size, [upload progress](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/asset-uploads)
 and [download progress](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/persistance)
 of that asset.

### Layer alignment[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#layer-alignment "Direct link to Layer alignment")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_ALIGNMENT_CONTROL`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_ALIGNMENT_CONTROL&type=code)

Buttons for aligning the item to the; left, vertical center, right, top, horizontal center, bottom.

### Position controls[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#position-controls "Direct link to Position controls")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_POSITION_CONTROL`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_POSITION_CONTROL&type=code)

Allows setting the precise X and Y position of the item.

### Dimensions controls[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#dimensions-controls "Direct link to Dimensions controls")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_DIMENSIONS_CONTROL`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_DIMENSIONS_CONTROL&type=code)

Allows setting the width and height of the item.

### Cropping controls[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#cropping-controls "Direct link to Cropping controls")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_CROP_CONTROL`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_CROP_CONTROL&type=code)

Allows setting the [crop values](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/cropping)
 of the item.

### Keep Aspect ratio control[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#keep-aspect-ratio-control "Direct link to Keep Aspect ratio control")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_KEEP_ASPECT_RATIO_CONTROL`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_KEEP_ASPECT_RATIO_CONTROL&type=code)

Allows setting the keep aspect ratio of the item.

### Border radius control[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#border-radius-control "Direct link to Border radius control")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_BORDER_RADIUS_CONTROL`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_BORDER_RADIUS_CONTROL&type=code)

Allows setting the border radius of the item. In the UI, the border radius is named "Corner Radius".

### Opacity control[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#opacity-control "Direct link to Opacity control")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_OPACITY_CONTROL`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_OPACITY_CONTROL&type=code)

Allows setting the opacity of the item.

### Text alignment[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#text-alignment "Direct link to Text alignment")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_TEXT_ALIGNMENT_CONTROL`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_TEXT_ALIGNMENT_CONTROL&type=code)

Allows setting the text alignment of the item (left, center, right).

### Font family[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#font-family "Direct link to Font family")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_FONT_FAMILY_CONTROL`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_FONT_FAMILY_CONTROL&type=code)

Allows setting the font family of the item.

### Font family preview[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#font-family-preview "Direct link to Font family preview")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_FONT_FAMILY_DROPDOWN_RENDER_IN_FONT`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_FONT_FAMILY_DROPDOWN_RENDER_IN_FONT&type=code)

Each item in the font family dropdown is rendered in the font itself.

### Hover to preview font family[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#hover-to-preview-font-family "Direct link to Hover to preview font family")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_CHANGE_FONT_FAMILY_ON_HOVER`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter+FEATURE_CHANGE_FONT_FAMILY_ON_HOVER&type=code)

When hovering over an item in the font family dropdown, the text item in the canvas updates with a preview of what the item would look like if it the hovered item was selected.

### Font style[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#font-style "Direct link to Font style")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_FONT_STYLE_CONTROL`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_FONT_STYLE_CONTROL&type=code)

Allows setting the font style variant and weight (normal, italic, bold, etc.) for text and caption items. Includes live preview when hovering over options.

### Hover to preview font style[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#hover-to-preview-font-style "Direct link to Hover to preview font style")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_CHANGE_FONT_STYLE_ON_HOVER`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter+FEATURE_CHANGE_FONT_STYLE_ON_HOVER&type=code)

When hovering over an item in the font style dropdown, the text item in the canvas updates with a preview of what the item would look like if it the hovered item was selected.

### Font size[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#font-size "Direct link to Font size")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_TEXT_FONT_SIZE_CONTROL`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_TEXT_FONT_SIZE_CONTROL&type=code)

Allows setting the font size of the item.

### Text value[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#text-value "Direct link to Text value")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_TEXT_VALUE_CONTROL`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_TEXT_VALUE_CONTROL&type=code)

Allows editing the text content via a text area in the inspector. Text area automatically resizes based on content and alignment settings.

### Color[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#color "Direct link to Color")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_COLOR_CONTROL`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_COLOR_CONTROL&type=code)

Provides a color picker for text, solid, and caption items.

### Rotation[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#rotation "Direct link to Rotation")

Feature flags: [`FEATURE_ROTATION_CONTROL`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_ROTATION_CONTROL&type=code)
 and [`FEATURE_ROTATE_90_DEGREES_BUTTON`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_ROTATE_90_DEGREES_BUTTON&type=code)

Allows changing the rotation of the item.

The [`FEATURE_ROTATE_90_DEGREES_BUTTON`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_ROTATE_90_DEGREES_BUTTON&type=code)
 flag displays a button to quickly rotate the item by 90 degrees clockwise.

### Line height[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#line-height "Direct link to Line height")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_TEXT_LINE_HEIGHT_CONTROL`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_TEXT_LINE_HEIGHT_CONTROL&type=code)

Allows setting the line height for text and caption items.  
Accepts values from 0.5 to 5.0 with automatic text relayout when changed.

### Letter spacing[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#letter-spacing "Direct link to Letter spacing")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_TEXT_LETTER_SPACING_CONTROL`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_TEXT_LETTER_SPACING_CONTROL&type=code)

Allows setting the letter spacing for text and caption items.  
Supports values from -10px to 50px with automatic text relayout when changed.

### Text direction[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#text-direction "Direct link to Text direction")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_TEXT_DIRECTION_CONTROL`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_TEXT_DIRECTION_CONTROL&type=code)

Provides left-to-right (LTR) and right-to-left (RTL; like Arabic) text direction controls.  
Text direction is automatically detected when creating new text items, but can be manually overridden.

### Playback speed[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#playback-speed "Direct link to Playback speed")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_PLAYBACKRATE_CONTROL`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_PLAYBACKRATE_CONTROL&type=code)

Allows setting the playback rate for video, audio, and GIF items.  
Supports rates from 0.25x to 5x with automatic duration adjustment based on the new playback speed.

### Volume[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#volume "Direct link to Volume")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_VOLUME_CONTROL`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_VOLUME_CONTROL&type=code)

Provides a volume slider for audio and video items with decibel-based adjustment.  
Shows the current dB difference to the original audio.

### Fade in and out[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#fade-in-and-out-1 "Direct link to Fade in and out")

Feature flags: [`FEATURE_AUDIO_FADE_CONTROL`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_AUDIO_FADE_CONTROL&type=code)
 and [`FEATURE_VIDEO_FADE_CONTROL`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_VIDEO_FADE_CONTROL&type=code)

Provides precise controls for fade in and fade out durations for audio and video items.  
Allows setting fade times in seconds with visual preview of the fade effect.

### Caption tokens[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#caption-tokens "Direct link to Caption tokens")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_TOKENS_CONTROL`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_TOKENS_CONTROL&type=code)

A control allowing the correction of the tokens of generated captions.

### Caption page duration[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#caption-page-duration "Direct link to Caption page duration")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_CAPTIONS_PAGE_DURATION_CONTROL`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_CAPTIONS_PAGE_DURATION_CONTROL&type=code)

A control allowing to set the duration of one page of captions.

### Lines of caption text[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#lines-of-caption-text "Direct link to Lines of caption text")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_TEXT_MAX_LINES_CONTROL`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_TEXT_MAX_LINES_CONTROL&type=code)

Limits the number of lines of caption text that can be displayed in a text item.

### Caption highlight color[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#caption-highlight-color "Direct link to Caption highlight color")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_CAPTIONS_HIGHLIGHT_COLOR_CONTROL`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_CAPTIONS_HIGHLIGHT_COLOR_CONTROL&type=code)

A control allowing to set the color of the currently spoken word in a caption page.

### Text background[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#text-background "Direct link to Text background")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_TEXT_BACKGROUND_CONTROL`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_TEXT_BACKGROUND_CONTROL&type=code)

A control allowing to set the background color of a text item.

### Text background horizontal padding[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#text-background-horizontal-padding "Direct link to Text background horizontal padding")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_TEXT_BACKGROUND_HORIZONTAL_PADDING_CONTROL`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_TEXT_BACKGROUND_HORIZONTAL_PADDING_CONTROL&type=code)

A control allowing to set the horizontal padding of the background of a text item.

### Text background border radius[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#text-background-border-radius "Direct link to Text background border radius")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_TEXT_BACKGROUND_BORDER_RADIUS_CONTROL`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_TEXT_BACKGROUND_BORDER_RADIUS_CONTROL&type=code)

A control allowing to set the border radius of the background of a text item.

Playback[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#playback "Direct link to Playback")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Play / Pause button[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#play--pause-button "Direct link to Play / Pause button")

A central play/pause button that starts or stops the video playback. It can also be controlled using the Space bar on the keyboard.

### Current time indicator[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#current-time-indicator "Direct link to Current time indicator")

Displays the current playhead position in timecode format (MM:SS.FF, where FF is the frame number).

### Jump to start button[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#jump-to-start-button "Direct link to Jump to start button")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_JUMP_TO_START_BUTTON`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_JUMP_TO_START_BUTTON&type=code)

Shows a button to jump to the start of the video.

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_JUMP_TO_END_BUTTON`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_JUMP_TO_END_BUTTON&type=code)

Shows a button to jump to the end of the video.

### Fullscreen button[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#fullscreen-button "Direct link to Fullscreen button")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_FULLSCREEN_CONTROL`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_FULLSCREEN_CONTROL&type=code)

Allows entering fullscreen mode for the video player (exiting with Esc).

### Mute button[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#mute-button "Direct link to Mute button")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_MUTE_BUTTON`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_MUTE_BUTTON&type=code)

Global mute/unmute switcher for the entire timeline audio.

### Loop[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#loop "Direct link to Loop")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_LOOP_BUTTON`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_LOOP_BUTTON&type=code)

Switcher button to enable or disable looping of the entire timeline.

### Zoom slider[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#zoom-slider "Direct link to Zoom slider")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_TIMELINE_ZOOM_SLIDER`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_TIMELINE_ZOOM_SLIDER&type=code)

Displays a slider at the right-side, just above the timeline, allowing to zoom in and out of the timeline.

### Resize timeline[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#resize-timeline "Direct link to Resize timeline")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_RESIZE_TIMELINE_PANEL`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_RESIZE_TIMELINE_PANEL&type=code)

Allows resizing the timeline panel by dragging the top edge of the panel.

Canvas[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#canvas "Direct link to Canvas")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Click to select[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#click-to-select "Direct link to Click to select")

Clicking on items in the canvas selects them. Selected items show a blue outline and can be edited via the inspector. Clicking on empty space deselects all items.

### Move and resize[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#move-and-resize "Direct link to Move and resize")

Selected items can be dragged to move their position on the canvas.  
Resize handles appear on selected items allowing for width and height adjustment by dragging the corners or edges.

### Shift axis lock[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#shift-axis-lock "Direct link to Shift axis lock")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_SHIFT_AXIS_LOCK`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_SHIFT_AXIS_LOCK&type=code)

While holding Shift when dragging; constrains item movement to horizontal or vertical axis only, helping with precise positioning and alignment.

### Shift aspect ratio lock[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#shift-aspect-ratio-lock "Direct link to Shift aspect ratio lock")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_SHIFT_KEY_TO_OVERRIDE_ASPECT_RATIO_LOCK`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_SHIFT_KEY_TO_OVERRIDE_ASPECT_RATIO_LOCK&type=code)
 (_disabled by default_)

While holding Shift when resizing video items; overrides the original aspect ratio, enabling distortion when scaling video items up or down.  
This feature is disabled by default.

### Multi-select / Marquee selection[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#multi-select--marquee-selection-1 "Direct link to Multi-select / Marquee selection")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_CANVAS_MARQUEE_SELECTION`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_CANVAS_MARQUEE_SELECTION&type=code)

Multiple items can be selected using Cmd/Ctrl+click (alternatively Shift+click) to add items to the selection, or so called Marquee selection to create a selection rectangle that selects all items within its bounds. Selected items can be moved, deleted, or edited together.

### Dropping assets[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#dropping-assets-1 "Direct link to Dropping assets")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_DROP_ASSETS_ON_CANVAS`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_DROP_ASSETS_ON_CANVAS&type=code)

Assets can be dropped directly onto the canvas from the file system.  
The item is automatically positioned to start at the current time and at the position in the canvas where it was dropped.

### Zoom controls[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#zoom-controls "Direct link to Zoom controls")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_CANVAS_ZOOM_CONTROLS`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_CANVAS_ZOOM_CONTROLS&type=code)

Shows an interface at the top right of the editor to zoom in and out within the canvas.  
The zoom level on the canvas is displayed as a percentage and can be reset to fit the available space.

### Zoom gestures[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#zoom-gestures "Direct link to Zoom gestures")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_CANVAS_ZOOM_GESTURES`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_CANVAS_ZOOM_GESTURES&type=code)

On desktop, holding Cmd/Ctrl while scrolling the mouse wheel zooms in and out of the canvas, with the mouse position as the zoom center.

On touch devices, pinch gestures zoom into the canvas.

### Zoom keyboard shortcuts[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#zoom-keyboard-shortcuts "Direct link to Zoom keyboard shortcuts")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_CANVAS_ZOOM_KEYBOARD_SHORTCUTS`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_CANVAS_ZOOM_KEYBOARD_SHORTCUTS&type=code)

You can press + and \- to zoom in and out.  
Press 0 to reset the zoom.

### Copy and paste[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#copy-and-paste "Direct link to Copy and paste")

Feature flags: [`FEATURE_COPY_PASTE_LAYERS`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_COPY_PASTE_LAYERS&type=code)
 and [`FEATURE_PASTE_ASSETS`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_PASTE_ASSETS&type=code)

Items can be copied with Cmd/Ctrl+C and pasted with Cmd/Ctrl+V.  
Pasted items are automatically positioned and can include text, images, and other assets from the clipboard.

### Bring to front / Send to back[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#bring-to-front--send-to-back "Direct link to Bring to front / Send to back")

Feature flags: [`FEATURE_BRING_TO_FRONT`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_BRING_TO_FRONT&type=code)
 and [`FEATURE_SEND_TO_BACK`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_SEND_TO_BACK&type=code)

Right-clicking on an item shows a menu with options to bring the item to front or send it to back.

### Canvas snapping[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#canvas-snapping "Direct link to Canvas snapping")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_CANVAS_SNAPPING`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_CANVAS_SNAPPING&type=code)

Enables snapping on the canvas, allowing items to snap to the edges of the canvas or the center of the canvas, both horizontally and vertically, when dragged. This simplifies positioning and transitions. Activate snapping by clicking the magnet icon.

Cropping[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#cropping "Direct link to Cropping")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_CROPPING`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_CROPPING&type=code)

Enables [cropping](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/cropping)
 functionality.

### Double-click to crop[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#double-click-to-crop "Direct link to Double-click to crop")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_DOUBLE_CLICK_TO_CROP`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_DOUBLE_CLICK_TO_CROP&type=code)

By double-clicking an item that is croppable on the canvas, [Crop Mode](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/cropping#crop-mode)
 is activated, replacing resize handles with crop handles.

### Crop backgrounds[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#crop-backgrounds "Direct link to Crop backgrounds")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_CROP_BACKGROUNDS`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_CROP_BACKGROUNDS&type=code)

When in [Crop Mode](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/cropping#crop-mode)
, the full uncropped item is shown in the background with reduced opacity, to help the user visualize the cutout area.

Behaviors[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#behaviors "Direct link to Behaviors")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Cut, copy, paste, duplicate items[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#cut-copy-paste-duplicate-items "Direct link to Cut, copy, paste, duplicate items")

Feature flags: [`FEATURE_CUT_LAYERS`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_CUT_LAYERS&type=code)
, [`FEATURE_COPY_LAYERS`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_COPY_LAYERS&type=code)
, and [`FEATURE_DUPLICATE_LAYERS`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_DUPLICATE_LAYERS&type=code)

Standard cut (Cmd/Ctrl+X), copy (Cmd/Ctrl+C), and paste (Cmd/Ctrl+V) operations work with items.  
Duplicate (Cmd/Ctrl+D) creates copies of selected items.

### Paste text[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#paste-text "Direct link to Paste text")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_PASTE_TEXT`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_PASTE_TEXT&type=code)

When pasting and there is text in the clipboard, the text is pasted as a text item.

### Paste assets[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#paste-assets "Direct link to Paste assets")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_PASTE_ASSETS`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_PASTE_ASSETS&type=code)

When pasting and there is an asset in the clipboard, the asset is pasted as a new item.  
Due to browser support, this may only work with images.

### Save and load[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#save-and-load "Direct link to Save and load")

Feature flags: [`FEATURE_SAVE_BUTTON`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_SAVE_BUTTON&type=code)
 and [`FEATURE_SAVE_SHORTCUT`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_SAVE_SHORTCUT&type=code)

A save can be triggered with Cmd/Ctrl+S. When a project is saved, it will be automatically loaded when returning to the editor.

### Load state from URL[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#load-state-from-url "Direct link to Load state from URL")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_LOAD_STATE_FROM_URL`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_LOAD_STATE_FROM_URL&type=code)

Allows loading the initial state of the editor from a URL hash `#state=[value]`.

The `[value]` must be a base64-encoded serialization of [`UndoableState`](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/state-management#undoable-state)
.

### Asset uploads[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#asset-uploads "Direct link to Asset uploads")

Assets are uploaded and processed when dropped into the editor. Upload progress is shown with indicators, and assets are stored for use across browser sessions. Multiple file formats are supported.

### Undo and redo[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#undo-and-redo "Direct link to Undo and redo")

Feature flags: [`FEATURE_UNDO_SHORTCUT`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_UNDO_SHORTCUT&type=code)
 and [`FEATURE_REDO_SHORTCUT`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_REDO_SHORTCUT&type=code)

Use Cmd/Ctrl+Z to undo and Cmd/Ctrl+Y or Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+Z to redo actions.

### Snapping toggle shortcut[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#snapping-toggle-shortcut "Direct link to Snapping toggle shortcut")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_SNAPPING_SHORTCUT`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_SNAPPING_SHORTCUT&type=code)

Allows toggling timeline and canvas snapping on/off using the keyboard shortcut Shift+M. When this feature is enabled, you can temporarily (de)activate snapping using the shortcut.

### Asset caching[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#asset-caching "Direct link to Asset caching")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_CACHE_ASSETS_LOCALLY`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_CACHE_ASSETS_LOCALLY&type=code)

[Assets are cached locally](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/persistance)
 in the browser using IndexedDB for faster loading and offline editing capabilities. Cached assets persist across browser sessions.

### Warn on leave when long-running tasks are in progress[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#warn-on-leave-when-long-running-tasks-are-in-progress "Direct link to Warn on leave when long-running tasks are in progress")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_WARN_ON_LONG_RUNNING_PROCESS_IN_PROGRESS`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_WARN_ON_LONG_RUNNING_PROCESS_IN_PROGRESS&type=code)

Asks the user for confirmation when leaving the page while a render, asset upload or caption task is in progress.

Captioning[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#captioning "Direct link to Captioning")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Transcription[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#transcription "Direct link to Transcription")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_CAPTIONING`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_CAPTIONING&type=code)

Enables UI for transcribing audio and video assets to captions.  
See: [Captioning](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/captioning)

Rendering[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#rendering "Direct link to Rendering")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Render button[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#render-button "Direct link to Render button")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_RENDERING`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_RENDERING&type=code)

Enables UI for [rendering](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/rendering)
 the current composition to a video file. Shows render progress and allows download of the final rendered video.

### Codec selector[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#codec-selector "Direct link to Codec selector")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_RENDERING_CODEC_SELECTOR`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_RENDERING_CODEC_SELECTOR&type=code)

Allows selecting the codec (MP4 / WebM) for rendering.

Media[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#media "Direct link to Media")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Enable new Media tags[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#enable-new-media-tags "Direct link to Enable new Media tags")

Feature flag: [`FEATURE_NEW_MEDIA_TAGS`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_NEW_MEDIA_TAGS&type=code)

Enables the new [`<Video>`](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/media/video)
 and [`<Audio>`](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/media/audio)
 tags.  
Leads to smoother playback and faster rendering, [but may have some rough edges](https://github.com/remotion-dev/remotion/issues/5650)
.

Keyboard shortcuts[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/editor-starter/features#keyboard-shortcuts "Direct link to Keyboard shortcuts")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

|     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- |
| ⌘/Ctrl<br><br>Z | Undo | [`FEATURE_UNDO_SHORTCUT`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_UNDO_SHORTCUT&type=code) |
| ⌘/Ctrl<br><br>Y  <br>⌘/Ctrl<br><br>Shift<br><br>Z | Redo | [`FEATURE_REDO_SHORTCUT`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_REDO_SHORTCUT&type=code) |
|     | 1 frame forward |     |
|     | 1 frame backwards |     |
| +   | Zoom in | [`FEATURE_CANVAS_ZOOM_KEYBOARD_SHORTCUTS`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_CANVAS_ZOOM_KEYBOARD_SHORTCUTS&type=code) |
| \-  | Zoom out | [`FEATURE_CANVAS_ZOOM_KEYBOARD_SHORTCUTS`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_CANVAS_ZOOM_KEYBOARD_SHORTCUTS&type=code) |
| 0   | Reset zoom | [`FEATURE_CANVAS_ZOOM_KEYBOARD_SHORTCUTS`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_CANVAS_ZOOM_KEYBOARD_SHORTCUTS&type=code) |
| Space | Play / Pause |     |
| ⌘/Ctrl<br><br>S | Save | [`FEATURE_SAVE_BUTTON`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_SAVE_BUTTON&type=code) |
| Hold Shift or ⌘/Ctrl | While selecting items (multi-select) |     |
| Hold Shift while dragging item | Constrain movement to horizontal/vertical axis only | [`FEATURE_SHIFT_AXIS_LOCK`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_SHIFT_AXIS_LOCK&type=code) |
| ⌘/Ctrl<br><br>A | Select all items | [`FEATURE_SELECT_ALL_SHORTCUT`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_SELECT_ALL_SHORTCUT&type=code) |
| ⌘/Ctrl<br><br>X | Cut selected item(s) | [`FEATURE_CUT_LAYERS`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_CUT_LAYERS&type=code) |
| ⌘/Ctrl<br><br>C | Copy selected item(s) | [`FEATURE_COPY_PASTE_LAYERS`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_COPY_PASTE_LAYERS&type=code) |
| ⌘/Ctrl<br><br>V | Paste item(s) | [`FEATURE_COPY_PASTE_LAYERS`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_COPY_PASTE_LAYERS&type=code) |
| ⌘/Ctrl<br><br>D | Duplicate selected item(s) | [`FEATURE_DUPLICATE_LAYERS`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_DUPLICATE_LAYERS&type=code) |
| Delete or Backspace | Delete selected item(s) | [`FEATURE_BACKSPACE_TO_DELETE`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_BACKSPACE_TO_DELETE&type=code) |
| Shift<br><br>M | Toggle timeline and canvas snapping | [`FEATURE_SNAPPING_SHORTCUT`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aremotion-dev%2Feditor-starter%20FEATURE_SNAPPING_SHORTCUT&type=code) |

# @remotion/captions | Remotion | Make videos programmatically

[Skip to main content](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/captions/api#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Copy page

On this page

_Available from v4.0.216_

The `@remotion/captions` package provides utilities for dealing with subtitles.

At the centre of this caption is the [`Caption`](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/captions/caption)
 type, which defines a standard shape for captions from different sources.

Captions can be generated from several sources and converted into the `Caption` type:

*   [`@remotion/install-whisper-cpp`](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/install-whisper-cpp)
     — using [`toCaptions()`](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/install-whisper-cpp/to-captions)
    
*   [`@remotion/whisper-web`](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/whisper-web)
     — using [`toCaptions()`](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/whisper-web/to-captions)
    
*   [`@remotion/openai-whisper`](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/openai-whisper)
     — using [`openAiWhisperApiToCaptions()`](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/openai-whisper/openai-whisper-api-to-captions)
    
*   [`@remotion/elevenlabs`](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/elevenlabs)
     — using [`elevenLabsTranscriptToCaptions()`](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/elevenlabs/elevenlabs-transcript-to-captions)
    

*   Remotion CLI
*   npm
*   bun
*   pnpm
*   yarn

npx remotion add @remotion/captions

Copy

npm i --save-exact @remotion/[\[email protected\]](https://www.remotion.dev/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection)

Copy

This assumes you are currently using v4.0.448 of Remotion.  
Also update `remotion` and all `` `@remotion/*` `` packages to the same version.  
Remove all `^` character in front of the version numbers of it as it can lead to a version conflict.

pnpm i @remotion/[\[email protected\]](https://www.remotion.dev/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection)

Copy

This assumes you are currently using v4.0.448 of Remotion.  
Also update `remotion` and all `` `@remotion/*` `` packages to the same version.  
Remove all `^` character in front of the version numbers of it as it can lead to a version conflict.

bun i @remotion/[\[email protected\]](https://www.remotion.dev/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection)

Copy

This assumes you are currently using v4.0.448 of Remotion.  
Also update `remotion` and all `` `@remotion/*` `` packages to the same version.  
Remove all `^` character in front of the version numbers of it as it can lead to a version conflict.

yarn --exact add @remotion/[\[email protected\]](https://www.remotion.dev/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection)

Copy

This assumes you are currently using v4.0.448 of Remotion.  
Also update `remotion` and all `` `@remotion/*` `` packages to the same version.  
Remove all `^` character in front of the version numbers of it as it can lead to a version conflict.

APIs[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/captions/api#apis "Direct link to APIs")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[**Caption**\
\
An object shape for captions](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/captions/caption)
[**parseSrt()**\
\
Parse a .srt file into a `Caption` array](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/captions/parse-srt)
[**serializeSrt()**\
\
Serialize a .srt file into a `Caption` array](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/captions/serialize-srt)
[**createTikTokStyleCaptions()**\
\
Structure the captions for TikTok-style display](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/captions/create-tiktok-style-captions)

License[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/captions/api#license "Direct link to License")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

MIT

*   [APIs](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/captions/api#apis)
    
*   [License](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/captions/api#license)

---

# Render your video | Remotion | Make videos programmatically

[Skip to main content](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/render#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Copy page

On this page

There are various ways to render your video.

Remotion Studio[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/render#remotion-studio "Direct link to Remotion Studio")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

To render a video, click the " Render" button.  
Choose your preferred settings, then confirm using the `Render video` button.

### Remotion Studio deployment[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/render#remotion-studio-deployment "Direct link to Remotion Studio deployment")

It’s possible to deploy the Remotion Studio onto a long-running server in the cloud, which can then be accessed by your non-technical team members using just a URL. Check out the [Deploy the Remotion Studio](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/studio/deploy-server)
 guide to learn how to do this.

CLI[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/render#cli "Direct link to CLI")

----------------------------------------------------------------------

Render a video using [`render` CLI command](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/cli/render)
:

    npx remotion render HelloWorld

Modify the composition ID to select a different video to render, or add an output path at the end if you want to override the default.

You can leave out the composition name and a picker will be shown:

    npx remotion render

SSR[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/render#ssr "Direct link to SSR")

----------------------------------------------------------------------

Remotion has a full-featured server-side rendering API. Read more about it on the [server-side rendering API](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/ssr)
.

AWS Lambda[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/render#aws-lambda "Direct link to AWS Lambda")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Check out [Remotion Lambda](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/lambda)
.

GitHub Actions[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/render#github-actions "Direct link to GitHub Actions")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can also render a [video using a GitHub Action.](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/ssr#render-using-github-actions)

Google Cloud Run[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/render#google-cloud-run "Direct link to Google Cloud Run")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

An alpha version of Remotion for Cloud Run is [available](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/cloudrun)
.  
We plan to change it in the future so it shares a runtime with Remotion Lambda.

Variants[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/render#variants "Direct link to Variants")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Audio-only[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/render#audio-only "Direct link to Audio-only")

Instead of rendering a video, [you can also just export the audio.](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/encoding#audio-only-export)

### Image Sequence[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/render#image-sequence "Direct link to Image Sequence")

Instead of encoding as a video, you can use the [`--sequence`](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/cli/render#--sequence)
 command to output a series of image.

### Still images[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/render#still-images "Direct link to Still images")

If you want a single image, you can do so using [the CLI or Node.JS API](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/stills)
.

### GIF[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/render#gif "Direct link to GIF")

See: [Render as GIF](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/render-as-gif)

### Transparent videos[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/render#transparent-videos "Direct link to Transparent videos")

See: [Creating overlays](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/overlay)

### See also[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/render#see-also "Direct link to See also")

*   [CLI options](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/cli)
    

*   [Remotion Studio](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/render#remotion-studio)
    *   [Remotion Studio deployment](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/render#remotion-studio-deployment)
        
*   [CLI](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/render#cli)
    
*   [SSR](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/render#ssr)
    
*   [AWS Lambda](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/render#aws-lambda)
    
*   [GitHub Actions](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/render#github-actions)
    
*   [Google Cloud Run](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/render#google-cloud-run)
    
*   [Variants](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/render#variants)
    *   [Audio-only](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/render#audio-only)
        
    *   [Image Sequence](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/render#image-sequence)
        
    *   [Still images](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/render#still-images)
        
    *   [GIF](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/render#gif)
        
    *   [Transparent videos](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/render#transparent-videos)
        
    *   [See also](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/render#see-also)

---

# @remotion/cloudrun | Remotion | Make videos programmatically

[Skip to main content](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/cloudrun/api#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Copy page

On this page

EXPERIMENTAL

Cloud Run is in [Alpha status and not actively being developed.](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/cloudrun/status)

*   Remotion CLI
*   npm
*   bun
*   pnpm
*   yarn

npx remotion add @remotion/cloudrun

Copy

npm i --save-exact @remotion/[\[email protected\]](https://www.remotion.dev/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection)

Copy

This assumes you are currently using v4.0.448 of Remotion.  
Also update `remotion` and all `` `@remotion/*` `` packages to the same version.  
Remove all `^` character in front of the version numbers of it as it can lead to a version conflict.

pnpm i @remotion/[\[email protected\]](https://www.remotion.dev/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection)

Copy

This assumes you are currently using v4.0.448 of Remotion.  
Also update `remotion` and all `` `@remotion/*` `` packages to the same version.  
Remove all `^` character in front of the version numbers of it as it can lead to a version conflict.

bun i @remotion/[\[email protected\]](https://www.remotion.dev/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection)

Copy

This assumes you are currently using v4.0.448 of Remotion.  
Also update `remotion` and all `` `@remotion/*` `` packages to the same version.  
Remove all `^` character in front of the version numbers of it as it can lead to a version conflict.

yarn --exact add @remotion/[\[email protected\]](https://www.remotion.dev/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection)

Copy

This assumes you are currently using v4.0.448 of Remotion.  
Also update `remotion` and all `` `@remotion/*` `` packages to the same version.  
Remove all `^` character in front of the version numbers of it as it can lead to a version conflict.

  

**See the [setup guide](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/cloudrun/setup)
 for complete instructions on how to get started.**

APIs[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/cloudrun/api#apis "Direct link to APIs")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The following Node.JS are available:

[**getServiceInfo()**\
\
Gets information about a service](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/cloudrun/getserviceinfo)
[**deployService()**\
\
Create a new service in GCP Cloud Run](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/cloudrun/deployservice)
[**deleteService()**\
\
Delete a service in GCP Cloud Run](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/cloudrun/deleteservice)
[**getServices()**\
\
Lists available Remotion Cloud Run services](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/cloudrun/getservices)
[**speculateServiceName()**\
\
Speculate a service name based on its configuration](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/cloudrun/speculateservicename)
[**getRegions()**\
\
Get all available regions](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/cloudrun/getregions)
[**deploySite()**\
\
Bundle and upload a site to Cloud Storage](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/cloudrun/deploysite)
[**deleteSite()**\
\
Delete a bundle from Cloud Storage](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/cloudrun/deletesite)
[**getSites()**\
\
Get all available sites from Cloud Storage](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/cloudrun/getsites)
[**getOrCreateBucket()**\
\
Ensure a Remotion Cloud Storage bucket exists](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/cloudrun/getorcreatebucket)
[**renderMediaOnCloudrun()**\
\
Trigger a video or audio render](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/cloudrun/rendermediaoncloudrun)
[**renderStillOnCloudrun()**\
\
Trigger a still render](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/cloudrun/renderstilloncloudrun)
[**testPermissions()**\
\
Ensure permissions are correctly set up in GCP](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/cloudrun/testpermissions)

CLI[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/cloudrun/api#cli "Direct link to CLI")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------

See [here](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/cloudrun/cli)
 for a list of CLI commands.

*   [APIs](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/cloudrun/api#apis)
    
*   [CLI](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/cloudrun/api#cli)

# Server-Side Rendering | Remotion | Make videos programmatically

[Skip to main content](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/ssr#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Copy page

On this page

Remotion's rendering engine is built with Node.JS, which makes it easy to render a video in the cloud.

See the [Comparison of SSR options](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/compare-ssr)
 to help you decide which option is best for you.

Render a video on AWS Lambda[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/ssr#render-a-video-on-aws-lambda "Direct link to Render a video on AWS Lambda")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The fastest way to render videos in the cloud is to use [`@remotion/lambda`](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/lambda)
.

Render using Vercel Sandbox[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/ssr#render-using-vercel-sandbox "Direct link to Render using Vercel Sandbox")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The easiest way, especially for Vercel customers is to use [Vercel Sandbox](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/vercel-sandbox)
.

Render a video using Node.js APIs[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/ssr#render-a-video-using-nodejs-apis "Direct link to Render a video using Node.js APIs")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

We provide a set of APIs to render videos using Node.js and Bun.  
See an [example](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/ssr-node)
 or the [API reference](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/renderer)
 for more information.

Render using GitHub Actions[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/ssr#render-using-github-actions "Direct link to Render using GitHub Actions")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can render a video on GitHub actions. The following workflow assumes a composition ID of `MyComp`

    name: Render video
    on:
      workflow_dispatch:
    jobs:
      render:
        name: Render video
        runs-on: ubuntu-latest
        steps:
          - uses: actions/checkout@main
          - uses: actions/setup-node@main
          - run: npm i
          - run: npx remotion render MyComp out/video.mp4
          - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
            with:
              name: out.mp4
              path: out/video.mp4

### With input props[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/ssr#with-input-props "Direct link to With input props")

If you have props, you can ask for them using the GitHub Actions input fields.  
Here we assume a shape of `{titleText: string; titleColor: string}`.

    name: Render video
    on:
      workflow_dispatch:
        inputs:
          titleText:
            description: 'Which text should it say?'
            required: true
            default: 'Welcome to Remotion'
          titleColor:
            description: 'Which color should it be in?'
            required: true
            default: 'black'
    jobs:
      render:
        name: Render video
        runs-on: ubuntu-latest
        steps:
          - uses: actions/checkout@main
          - uses: actions/setup-node@main
          - run: npm i
          - run: echo $WORKFLOW_INPUT > input-props.json
            env:
              WORKFLOW_INPUT: ${{ toJson(github.event.inputs) }}
          - run: npx remotion render MyComp out/video.mp4 --props="./input-props.json"
          - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
            with:
              name: out.mp4
              path: out/video.mp4

[](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/ssr#1)

[1](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/ssr#1)

[](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/ssr#1)
Commit the template to a GitHub repository.  
[2](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/ssr#2)
On GitHub, click the `Actions` tab.  
[3](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/ssr#3)
Select the `Render video` workflow on the left.  
[4](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/ssr#4)
A `Run workflow` button should appear. Click it.  
[5](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/ssr#5)
Fill in the props of the root component and click `Run workflow`.  
[6](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/ssr#6)
After the rendering is finished, you can download the video under `Artifacts`.  

Note that running the workflow may incur costs. However, the workflow will only run if you actively trigger it.

See also: [Passing input props in GitHub Actions](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/passing-props#passing-input-props-in-github-actions)

Render a video using Docker[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/ssr#render-a-video-using-docker "Direct link to Render a video using Docker")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

See: [Dockerizing a Remotion project](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/docker)

Deploy to a cloud platform[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/ssr#deploy-to-a-cloud-platform "Direct link to Deploy to a cloud platform")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The following guides show how to deploy a Remotion rendering service using the [Node.js/Bun APIs](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/renderer)
 on various cloud platforms:

*   [Azure Container Apps](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/azure-container-apps)
      
    
*   [Cloudflare Containers](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/cloudflare-containers)
      
    

Render a video using GCP Cloud Run (Alpha)[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/ssr#render-a-video-using-gcp-cloud-run-alpha "Direct link to Render a video using GCP Cloud Run (Alpha)")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Check out the experimental [Cloud Run](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/cloudrun)
 package.  
Note: It not actively being developed - our plan is to port the Lambda runtime to Cloud Run instead of maintaining a separate implementation.

API reference[​](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/ssr#api-reference "Direct link to API reference")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[**getCompositions()**\
\
List available compositions](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/renderer/get-compositions)
[**selectComposition()**\
\
Get a composition](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/renderer/select-composition)
[**renderMedia()**\
\
Render a video or audio](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/renderer/render-media)
[**renderFrames()**\
\
Render a series of images](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/renderer/render-frames)
[**renderStill()**\
\
Render a single image](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/renderer/render-still)
[**stitchFramesToVideo()**\
\
Turn images into a video](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/renderer/stitch-frames-to-video)
[**openBrowser()**\
\
Open a Chrome browser to reuse across renders](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/renderer/open-browser)
[**ensureBrowser()**\
\
Open a Chrome browser to reuse across renders](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/renderer/ensure-browser)
[**makeCancelSignal()**\
\
Create token to later cancel a render](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/renderer/make-cancel-signal)
[**getVideoMetadata()**\
\
Get metadata from a video file in Node.js](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/renderer/get-video-metadata)
[**getSilentParts()**\
\
Obtain silent portions of a video or audio](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/renderer/get-silent-parts)
[**combineChunks()**\
\
Combine chunks of partial renders](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/renderer/combine-chunks)
[**ensureFfmpeg()**\
\
Check for ffmpeg binary and install if not existing](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/renderer/ensure-ffmpeg)
[**ensureFfprobe()**\
\
Check for ffprobe binary and install if not existing](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/renderer/ensure-ffprobe)
[**getCanExtractFramesFast()**\
\
Probes for fast extraction for <OffthreadVideo>](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/renderer/get-can-extract-frames-fast)

*   [Render a video on AWS Lambda](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/ssr#render-a-video-on-aws-lambda)
    
*   [Render using Vercel Sandbox](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/ssr#render-using-vercel-sandbox)
    
*   [Render a video using Node.js APIs](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/ssr#render-a-video-using-nodejs-apis)
    
*   [Render using GitHub Actions](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/ssr#render-using-github-actions)
    *   [With input props](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/ssr#with-input-props)
        
*   [Render a video using Docker](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/ssr#render-a-video-using-docker)
    
*   [Deploy to a cloud platform](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/ssr#deploy-to-a-cloud-platform)
    
*   [Render a video using GCP Cloud Run (Alpha)](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/ssr#render-a-video-using-gcp-cloud-run-alpha)
    
*   [API reference](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/ssr#api-reference)

# API overview | Remotion | Make videos programmatically

[Skip to main content](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/api#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Copy page

[**Command line**\
\
Reference for the `npx remotion` commands](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/cli)
[**Configuration file**\
\
Reference for the `remotion.config.ts` file](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/config)

remotion
--------

Core APIs: `useCurrentFrame()`, `interpolate()`, etc.

[**<Composition>**\
\
Define a video](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/composition)
[**<Still>**\
\
Define a still](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/still)
[**<Folder>**\
\
Organize compositions in the Studio sidebar](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/folder)
[**registerRoot()**\
\
Initialize a Remotion project](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/register-root)
[**useCurrentFrame()**\
\
Obtain the current time](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/use-current-frame)
[**useVideoConfig()**\
\
Get the duration, dimensions and FPS of a composition](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/use-video-config)
[**interpolate()**\
\
Map a range of values to another](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/interpolate)
[**spring()**\
\
Physics-based animation primitive](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/spring)
[**interpolateColors()**\
\
Map a range of values to colors](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/interpolate-colors)
[**measureSpring()**\
\
Determine the duration of a spring](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/measure-spring)
[**Easing**\
\
Customize animation curve of `interpolate()`](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/easing)
[**<Img>**\
\
Render an `<img>` tag and wait for it to load](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/img)
[**<Html5Video>**\
\
Synchronize a `<video>` with Remotion's time](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/html5-video)
[**<Html5Audio>**\
\
Synchronize `<audio>` with Remotion's time](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/html5-audio)
[**<OffthreadVideo>**\
\
Alternative to `<Html5Video>`](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/offthreadvideo)
[**<AnimatedImage>**\
\
Disply a GIF, AVIF or animated WebP image](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/animatedimage)
[**<IFrame>**\
\
Render an `<iframe>` tag and wait for it to load](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/iframe)
[**<Sequence>**\
\
Time-shifts it's children](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/sequence)
[**<Series>**\
\
Display contents after another](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/series)
[**<Freeze>**\
\
Freeze some content in time](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/freeze)
[**<Loop>**\
\
Play some content repeatedly](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/loop)
[**delayRender()**\
\
Block a render from continuing](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/delay-render)
[**continueRender()**\
\
Unblock a render](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/continue-render)
[**cancelRender()**\
\
Abort an error](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/cancel-render)
[**getInputProps()**\
\
Receive the user-defined input data](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/get-input-props)
[**getRemotionEnvironment()**\
\
Determine if you are currently previewing or rendering](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/get-remotion-environment)
[**staticFile()**\
\
Access file from `public/` folder](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/staticfile)
[**<AbsoluteFill>**\
\
Position content absolutely and in full size](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/absolute-fill)
[**VERSION**\
\
Get the current version of Remotion](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/version)

---------------

An experimental `<NewVideo />` tag for embedding videos.

\
WebCodecs-based tag for embedding videos](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/media/video)
[**<Audio>**\
\
WebCodecs-based tag for embedding audio](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/media/audio)

-----------------

Create a Webpack bundle from Node.JS

[**bundle()**\
\
Create a Webpack bundle](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/bundle)

----------------

Play a Remotion video in the browser.

[**<Player>**\
\
Embed a Remotion composition in a web app](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/player/player)
[**<Thumbnail>**\
\
Embed a still in a web app](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/player/thumbnail)

@remotion/lambda
----------------

Render videos and stills on AWS Lambda

[**estimatePrice()**\
\
Estimate the price of a render](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/lambda/estimateprice)
[**deployFunction()**\
\
Create a new function in AWS Lambda](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/lambda/deployfunction)
[**deleteFunction()**\
\
Delete a function in AWS Lambda](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/lambda/deletefunction)
[**getFunctionInfo()**\
\
Gets information about a function](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/lambda/getfunctioninfo)
[**getFunctions()**\
\
Lists available Remotion Lambda functions](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/lambda/getfunctions)
[**getCompositionsOnLambda()**\
\
Gets list of compositions inside a Lambda function](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/lambda/getcompositionsonlambda)
[**deleteSite()**\
\
Delete a bundle from S3](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/lambda/deletesite)
[**deploySite()**\
\
Bundle and upload a site to S3](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/lambda/deploysite)
[**getAwsClient()**\
\
Access the AWS SDK directly](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/lambda/getawsclient)
[**getRegions()**\
\
Get all available regions](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/lambda/getregions)
[**getSites()**\
\
Get all available sites](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/lambda/getsites)
[**downloadMedia()**\
\
Download a render artifact from S3](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/lambda/downloadmedia)
[**getUserPolicy()**\
\
Get the policy JSON for your AWS user](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/lambda/getuserpolicy)
[**getRolePolicy()**\
\
Get the policy JSON for your AWS role](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/lambda/getrolepolicy)
[**getOrCreateBucket()**\
\
Ensure a Remotion S3 bucket exists](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/lambda/getorcreatebucket)
[**getRenderProgress()**\
\
Query the progress of a render](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/lambda/getrenderprogress)
[**presignUrl()**\
\
Make a private file public to those with the link](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/lambda/presignurl)
[**renderMediaOnLambda()**\
\
Trigger a video or audio render](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/lambda/rendermediaonlambda)
[**renderStillOnLambda()**\
\
Trigger a still render](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/lambda/renderstillonlambda)
[**simulatePermissions()**\
\
Ensure permissions are correctly set up](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/lambda/simulatepermissions)
[**speculateFunctionName()**\
\
Get the lambda function name based on its configuration](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/lambda/speculatefunctionname)
[**validateWebhookSignature()**\
\
Validate an incoming webhook request is authentic](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/lambda/validatewebhooksignature)
[**appRouterWebhook()**\
\
Handle incoming webhooks specifically for the Next.js app router](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/lambda/approuterwebhook)
[**pagesRouterWebhook()**\
\
Handle incoming webhooks specifically for the Next.js pages router](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/lambda/pagesrouterwebhook)
[**expressWebhook()**\
\
Handle incoming webhooks specifically for Express.js](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/lambda/expresswebhook)

Common operations for subtitles.

An object shape for captions](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/captions/caption)

Parse a .srt file into a `Caption` array](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/captions/parse-srt)

Serialize a .srt file into a `Caption` array](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/captions/serialize-srt)

\
Structure the captions for TikTok-style display](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/captions/create-tiktok-style-captions)

Include a GIF in your video.

\
Render a GIF](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/gif/gif)

Get the runtime of a GIF](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/gif/get-gif-duration-in-seconds)

\
Prepare a GIF for displaying in the Player](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/gif/preload-gif)

---------------------

Obtain info about video and audio.

[**audioBufferToDataUrl()**\
\
Serialize an audio buffer](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/audio-buffer-to-data-url)
[**getAudioData()**\
\
Get metadata of an audio source](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/get-audio-data)

\
Get the duration of an audio source](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/get-audio-duration-in-seconds)

\
Get metadata of a video source](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/get-video-metadata)
[**getWaveformPortion()**\
\
Trims audio data into a waveform](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/get-waveform-portion)
[**useAudioData()**\
\
`getAudioData()` as a hook](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/use-audio-data)
[**useWindowedAudioData()**\
\
Optimized for fetching only current data, works only with `.wav`](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/use-windowed-audio-data)
[**visualizeAudio()**\
\
Process a music waveform for visualization](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/visualize-audio)
[**visualizeAudioWaveform()**\
\
Process a voice waveform for visualization](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/media-utils/visualize-audio-waveform)
[**createSmoothSvgPath()**\
\
Turn waveform points into a smooth SVG path](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/media-utils/create-smooth-svg-path)

@remotion/skia
--------------

Low-level graphics using React Native Skia

Manipulate and obtain info about SVG paths

Obtain length of an SVG path](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/paths/get-length)

Cut an SVG path at a specified length](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/paths/cut-path)

\
Get coordinates at a certain point of an SVG path](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/paths/get-point-at-length)

\
Gets tangents `x` and `y` of a point which is on an SVG path](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/paths/get-tangent-at-length)

\
Switch direction of an SVG path](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/paths/reverse-path)
[**normalizePath()**\
\
Replace relative with absolute coordinates](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/paths/normalize-path)
[**interpolatePath()**\
\
Interpolates between two SVG paths](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/paths/interpolate-path)
[**evolvePath()**\
\
Animate an SVG path](https://www.remotion.dev/docs/paths/evolve-path)
